$schema: RateGuard cross-language conformance vectors — token bucket.
description: One shared oracle for the token-bucket admission decision (allowed, remaining), replayed by all 3 SDKs (Go, Node.js, Python) against their own Store.Increment. Proves real behavioral parity, not just matching test counts. See AGENTS.md rule 1: 'same algorithm, same API, same behavior.'
formula: tokens = min(burst, tokens + elapsed_seconds * rps); allow if tokens >= n, consume n; idle bucket (>600000ms since last access) resets to a full burst before refilling.
known_gap: retry_after_ms rounding currently differs across SDKs (Go rounds up to the nearest whole second; Node/Python round up to the nearest whole millisecond, floored at 1000ms) — these vectors intentionally check only `allowed` and `remaining`, not `retry_after_ms`, until that's unified.

steps.note: ask for 10 with only 6 left — deny, no consumption
consume exactly the 6 remaining — allow, bucket now empty
partial refill: 0.3s * 10rps = 3 tokens, not capped by burst
refill would exceed burst — clamps to burst before consuming
idle >10min triggers the reset-to-full branch, not just a large refill
